title: centaurea filiformis subsp. ferulacea
intro: Centaurea filiformis subsp. ferulacea, also known as Knapweed or Hardheads, is a perennial flowering plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its tall stems and small, white or pinkish flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Its health benefits include treating digestive issues, such as bloating and gas, as well as relieving symptoms of allergies and skin conditions. Centaurea filiformis subsp. ferulacea can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in homeopathic remedies due to its perceived healing properties.
